mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a directional accuracy below 55% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports higher equity valuations over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a continuous floor for consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a negative or null correlation with 20-day SPX direction across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Convergence of quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales trends confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which reinforces earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between GDPC1 and RSAFS trends eliminates predictive power, with directional accuracy falling below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a supply-side contraction and manufacturing recession, which depresses corporate profit forecasts and yields negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances following a 1.0% industrial production drawdown.
